mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate profit margins, causing equity valuations to decline over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across durable goods and real estate sectors, driving aggregate demand growth that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and capacity utilization improvements, generating positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns during inventory drawdowns, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 215 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally consistently when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening without statistical artifacts, supporting higher corporate revenue expectations and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when labor force participation drops sharply, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels directly correlate with aggregate corporate earnings growth, as expanding economic output increases sales volumes and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP levels show no predictive relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the signal reverses direction during financial stress.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, it indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing and utility output that precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or predictive power vanishes during supply chain disruptions.
